The single most surprising finding in the report, Passel says, is that 22% of newly wed black men married women who were not black.
The new figure is six times the intermarriage rate of roughly 2% in 1960, says senior demographer Jeff Passel, the report's lead author.
"The face of the country is changing, " said Jeffrey Passel, demographer at the Pew Hispanic Center.
Their number grows by at least 500, 000 a year, reckons Jeffrey Passel of the Urban Institute.
